workflow_instructions.mode_overview: This mode creates and modifies IBM Business Automation Workflow coach widgets. It focuses on widget implementation, preview assets, widget metadata, and widget documentation. It does not package widgets into TWX artifacts.

step.details: Identify whether the user wants to create a new widget, update an existing widget, add preview assets, define configuration metadata, or document widget behavior.

Review nearby widgets before making changes. Prefer examples that are structurally similar to the requested widget, such as simple display widgets, list-based widgets, or widgets with business object definitions and events.
Stay within widget creation and modification work. If the request moves into TWX generation, packaging validation, or deployment, hand off to the package manager mode.

phase.name: analysis
phase.description: Analyze requirements and project patterns before editing

steps.step: Identify the widget name and target folder under widgets/
Check whether similar widgets already exist and can be used as references
Determine required and optional files for the requested behavior

phase.name: implementation
phase.description: Implement or update the widget files

steps.step: Create or update Layout.html with semantic structure and accessibility attributes

Create or update InlineCSS.css with scoped styling using Carbon Design System values (see themes/CARBON_THEME_VARIABLES.md). Use hardcoded values with inline comments documenting the corresponding theme variables, NOT LESS variable references
Create or update inlineJavascript.js with BAW data and option access patterns
Create or update config.json and business object files when the widget needs metadata or complex types

Create event handler files in widget/events/ folder ONLY when the widget requires specific BAW events (load, change, view, validate, unload). Not all widgets need event handlers - create only what's necessary for the widget's functionality
Create or update preview files when the widget should be testable outside the BAW runtime
Keep documentation aligned with the implemented behavior
Create a palette icon SVG (120x120px square) named {WidgetName}.svg in the widget folder for catalog display

phase.name: validation
phase.description: Verify the widget implementation is coherent

steps.step: Check naming consistency across widget folder, preview files, and metadata
Ensure documentation matches the actual widget inputs, options, and events
Confirm the mode stops at the packaging boundary

completion_criteria.criterion: The requested widget files have been created or updated consistently
The widget follows repository naming and structure conventions
Preview and documentation are aligned with the implementation when applicable
The response stays within widget-authoring scope and does not perform packaging work
